name: Supremes De Volaille a Blanc
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup White or brown stock or canned beef bouillon
1/4 cup Port, Meadera or dry white vermouth
Salt and white pepper to taste
recipeInstructions: Preheat oven to 400°F. Rub the chicken breasts with drops of lemon juice and sprinkle lightly with salt and white pepper. Heat butter in a casserole dish until foaming. Quickly roll the chicken in the butter, lay a round of waxed paper to fit the casserole on top of them and place in a hot oven. After 6 minutes, press tops of chicken with your finger; if they are still soft and squishy, return to oven for a minute or two more. They are done when they feel lightly springy and resilient; do not overcook. Remove casserole from oven, put chicken in a hot serving dish; cover and keep warm while making the sauce which will take 2 to 3 minutes. To make sauce: Turn a burner to high and pour the stock or bouillon and the wine into the casserole with the hot butter. Stirring the liquid constantly, boil down rapidly over high heat until lightly thickened. Season carefully with salt, pepper, and drops of lemon juice. Pour the sauce over the chicken, sprinkle with parsley and serve immediately.
